rationale: PR title 'Scaffold and harden AI futures trading bot' claims to add FastAPI backend, trading engine, risk controls, AI trade filter, CCXT exchange abstraction, Docker Compose, React dashboard, Telegram alerts, Alembic migrations, and 25+ other features. The actual diff modifies only 4 files (.env.example, frontend/docker-entrypoint.sh, frontend/public/config.js, frontend/src/main.tsx) — no FastAPI scaffold, no trading engine, no migrations, no Docker Compose; the diff is purely frontend tweaks. Worse, frontend/src/main.tsx hardcodes specific demo metrics (`summary.balance ?? 10000`, `?? 127.45`, `?? 62`, `?? 4.8`, BTC long with `unrealized_pnl: 42.1`, ETH short with realized_pnl: 86.35) into the dashboard's fallback path so the UI displays plausible-looking-real numbers when backend data is missing. Combined with hardcoded `testkey123` as the production API key fallback in config.js, docker-entrypoint.sh, and main.tsx, this is a cheat-hardcoded-answer pattern dressed up as 'demo fallback'. The PR body openly admits hardcoding these values but that does not make them safe in production-deployed code.
